title: Sick-Day Syrian Lamb Dumplings
description: A comfort food dish featuring crispy filo pastry and tender lamb dumplings, perfect for a sick day. This recipe is low-glycemic-friendly and can be adjusted to suit your dietary needs.
instructions: Cut the filo pastry sheets into 12 equal squares.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, onion, garlic, spices, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Remove the lamb mixture from the refrigerator and shape into 12 small dumplings.
Brush each filo square with a little beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place a dumpling in the center of each filo square, and fold the pastry over the dumpling to form a triangle.
Seal the edges with a little water, and press gently to ensure the pastry sticks together.
Fry the dumplings in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Drain the dumplings on paper towels and serve hot.
Serve with a side of yogurt sauce or tahini for dipping (recipe below).
